title: gRPC

topic.name: gRPC
topic.description: Comprehensive guide to gRPC protocol, Protocol Buffers, streaming types, performance optimization, and comparison with REST

quiz.question: What are the four types of gRPC communication patterns?
options: Unary, Server Streaming, Client Streaming, Bidirectional Streaming
quiz.explanation: gRPC supports four communication patterns: Unary (single request/response), Server Streaming (single request, stream of responses), Client Streaming (stream of requests, single response), and Bidirectional Streaming (stream of both).

quiz.question: Why is Protocol Buffers preferred over JSON for gRPC?
options: Protocol Buffers provide binary serialization which is more compact and faster to serialize/deserialize than JSON
Protocol Buffers support more data types
Protocol Buffers are only used with gRPC
quiz.explanation: Protocol Buffers use binary serialization which results in smaller message sizes and faster serialization/deserialization compared to JSON's text-based format. This is crucial for high-performance microservice communication.

quiz.question: When should you choose REST over gRPC?
options: When you need browser compatibility and public APIs with simple request-response patterns
quiz.explanation: REST is better suited for public APIs, browser-based applications, and scenarios where simplicity and broad tooling support are more important than raw performance. gRPC excels in internal microservice communication where performance is critical.
